name: rda-toolkit
documentation:
# rdatools
Redistricting analysis tools (RDA)

Companion repositories include:

* [rdatools/vtd_data](https://github.com/rdatools/vtd_data)
* [rdatools/vtd_ensembles](https://github.com/rdatools/vtd_ensembles)

modules.name: rdatools.base.requireargs
documentation: REQUIRE ARGS - DEBUG & EXPLICIT MODES

functions.name: rdatools.base.requireargs.require_args
documentation:
This extends parser.parse_args() to enable two modes of operation:
- debug: default values are used for the args in debug_defaults, regardless of user input or add_argument defaults
- explicit: values are required for the args in debug_defaults, whether from user input or add_argument defaults

functions.name: rdatools.base.requireargs.parse_args
documentation:
Examples:

$ ./requireargs.py                                                                 # debug defaults
$ ./requireargs.py --explicit                                                      # fails
$ ./requireargs.py --explicit --requiredstr foo --requiredint 42                   # works
$ ./requireargs.py --explicit --requiredstr foo --requiredint 42 --optionalint 100 # works

modules.name: rdatools.base.graph
documentation: ADJACENCY/CONTIGUITY GRAPHS

functions.name: rdatools.base.graph.is_connected
documentation:
Make sure a graph is fully connected *internally*, i.e., w/o regard to the virtual state boundary "shapes".

Kenshi's iterative implementation of the recursive algorithm

geos - the list of geographies
adjacency - the connectedness of the geos
## HELPERS ###

functions.name: rdatools.base.graph.read_mods
documentation:
Read a CSV file of modifications to a graph.

Example:

+, 440099902000, 440099901000

documentation: Make sure each node is in every neighbor's neighbor list

documentation: Remove a node from the graph maintaining its connectedness.

documentation: Bridge over a node, i.e., connect the neighbors directly.

methods.name: rdatools.base.graph.Graph.add_adjacency
documentation: Connect two nodes in the graph.

methods.name: rdatools.base.graph.Graph.adjacencies
documentation: Return unique pairs of adjacent nodes in the graph.
